docs.title: Welcome
docs.text:
Abriana Poola Datasheet as part of Submersible Exploration Device for Team 314

Preparation: March 6th, 2026

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
This purpose of this datasheet is to go over the HMI Module of the design and functionality of the OLED display and how what its role is in Team 307's Submersible Exploration Device. The document will gover the requirements that were set for this module and the layout of the component's pinouts. In addition, it will go over the final selections of components that are used for the final schematic of the module.

As a team member, my contributions to the team was to ensure assignments were submitted on time while ensuring the quality of the webpage is up to par. In addition to that, I communicated with the team about my module and discussed with Team 307 on how my subsystem will work with the submersible exploration device.

docs.title: Project Summary
docs.text:
The goal of this subsystem is to provide a HMI Module for the Team 307 which allows the user's the ability to interact with the device by controlling the speed of the machine with three options: Fast, neutral, stopping. In addition to that, the module will have the ability to give updated data to different boards while displaying it on screen.

  • Link to the Team 307's Report:

docs.title: My Contribution
docs.text:
• To review the details and relationships between the system’s components, refer to the Block Diagram section of the datasheet.
  • To review the system’s components, refer to the Component Selection.
  • To review the layout of the individual subsystem, refer to the Schematic.
  • To review the microcontroller's layout, refer to the Microcontroller.
  • To review power distribution, refer to the Power Budget.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The table below are requirements formed based on the needs of Team 307's project. This portion heavily focuses on the Human Machine Interface (HMI). In order to achieve this, I have decided to be focusing on the OLED module which allows users to interact with the machine through the screen. How users would interact with the machinery is by pushing serveral buttons to see the options and if they want to change the speed, they would have three options to choose from: fast, slow, stop. In addition to that, the users can see the data recieved from both the temperature/pressure sensor. The table also focuses on other aspects to make sure communication between teammates is achieved and that this fills the requirements individually.

docs.title: Module Requirement
docs.text: Requirement Description Measure of Threshold TargetMeasure StretchRequirement(Y-N) Surface mounted, 3.3V switching power regulatore 3.2 Volts 3.3 Volts No Surface mounted microcontroller 1 PIC or ESP 8-bit PIC No Wireless Communication Able to send or receive a Wi-Fi data Send and receive Wi-Fi Data to MQTT Yes OLED Screen Be able to showcase data Allows users to be able to see data by interacting with screen No

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The purpose of this block diagram is to showcase the components need to achieve the HMI portion of the team project. With that, there is the needed power source coming from a power adapter which is regulated by a voltage regulator, ensuring the board only recieves 3.3V. Furthermore, there is an LED as a test point for the project and push buttons which are digital inputs from the users to communicate on what they want to see from the OLED Screen. The OLED itself uses I2C communications which is why it is wired through GPIO15 and GPIO16. There is also a USB-B connector that is needed which is why it is connected into both GPIO19 and GPIO20 as on the datasheet those two GPIO's are the only ones who directly connect to the USB communicator.

docs.title: Module's Selected Major Components
docs.text: This page outlines the main components selected for the Human Machine Interface (HMI) module. The list includes power sources (barrel jacks), power regulators and OLEDs. These three are the main focuses of this page as the resistors, compacitors, leds, and headers are provided from the course.

docs.title: Choice 1: CONN PWR JACK 1.35X3.5MM SOLDER
docs.text:
• Price: $0.67/each
  • Product Link: Pj-007
Pros Cons Inexpensive compared to others Needs correct orientation Easy to install polarity needs to correct Already given CAD Model Familar

docs.title: Choice 2: Power Barrel Connector Jack 2.10mm ID (0.083"), 5.50mm OD (0.217") Surface Mount
docs.text:
• Price: $0.91/each
  • Product Link: PJ-006A-SMT-TR
Pros Cons Can handle temperature when soldering Expensive Is a Surface Mount Harder to place in PCB design Has 5,000 cycles of life ---

docs.title: Choice 3: CONN PWR JACK 2X5.5MM SOLDER
docs.text:
• Price: $0.69/each
  • Product Link: KLDX-0202-A
Pros Cons A TH component Orientation is different from classic Detailed Datasheet Different CAD Model Operating Temperature: -25˚C to +85˚C ---

docs.title: Selected Component
docs.text: The component selected for the power source is option 1: CONN PWR JACK 1.35X3.5MM SOLDER. The main reason is the pricing being significantly cheaper if ordered in bulk as this is a critical piece for the PCB. Furthermore, this item is given in class and something that we are familiar when working with it for other labs throughout the course.

docs.title: Choice 1: Linear 3.3V Voltage Regulator
docs.text:
• Price: $0.41/each
  • Product Link: L7809CV
Pros Cons Very simple to use A through-hole which we cannot use Linear Only 3 prongs which doesn't have the stuff required for the project --- DC-DC Conversion

docs.title: Choice 2: Switching Regulator IC Positive Fixed 3.3V
docs.text:
• Price: $0.22/each
  • Product Link: AP2112K-3.3TRG1
Pros Cons Better Performance Generates heat at high Voltage Cheap to order has a 600MA limit Can solder easily on board ---

docs.title: Choice 3: SMD 3.3V Power Regulator
docs.text:
• Price: $0.25/each
  • Product Link: MIC5528-3.3YMT-TR
Pros Cons Has protection for over current/temperature Out of the three options, it is slightly expensive Can handle -40°C ~ 125°C Can be difficult to install Saves space on the PCB Board ---

docs.title: Selected Component
docs.text: The selected component for this project is the second option: Switching Regulator IC Positive Fixed 3.3V. The reason why is because it has all the pinouts that are required for the main project and is inexpensive to order multiple for different scenerios. Furthermore, it would easier to implement in the PCB design and when soldering in-person.

docs.title: Choice 1: OLED LCD Display Board Module
docs.text:
• Price: Unknown
  • Product Link: OLED Screen
Pros Cons Familiar as it was a lab Currently unavalible to order pinouts are simple Screen is small Clear display Very coding heavy

docs.title: Choice 2: GRAPHIC DISPLAY STN WHITE (BLUE)
docs.text:
• Price: $10.95/each
  • Product Link: Graphic Display
Pros Cons Has a blue screen Expensive Multiple functions Has multiple pins that need to be connected --- Smaller screen than the first choice

docs.title: Choice 3: DISPLAY, COB 128x32 OLED, White
docs.text:
• Price: $11.89/each
  • Product Link: Digikey OLED
Pros Cons Can have input voltage around 2.6 ~ 5.2V Super Expensive Has chip select input Same screen length as option 2 Datasheet inlcuded Multiple Pinouts

docs.title: Selected Components:
docs.text: The component selected is option 1: OLED LCD Display Board Module. The reason why this was choosen was because it was a component given throughout the course which allowed us to learn more about this component. OLED screens are naturally expensive which is why the class one was selected for easy access.

docs.title: Summary Table of Final Selection
docs.text: Component Selection Rationale OLED Display OLED Screen This part was given in class which makes it easier to work with as all the programs that are needed are downloaded on the computer. Power Regulator AP2112K-3.3TRG1 Can be easily soldered onto the board and has the correct pinouts that are required for the course. It is also inexpensive while ensuring minimal noise for the I2C. Power Source Pj-007 It is the standard barreljack and can be easily soldered onto the board.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This page goes over the materials that are needed for the HMI Module to ensure. Some of the parts (ESP32, Power Regulator, Test Points) were ordered through Arizona State University while other parts were personally funded. The Bill of Materials is based off the schematic and the component selection to determine what parts were needed to order.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
This schematic is design to support the HMI Module of Team 307's Submersible Exploration Device. The goal of this module is to allow users to interact with the OLED by using push buttons to go through a series of menu options to recieve data or change the speed of the motor in three modes: Fast, slow, and stop. This satisfies the project's requirement of allowing users to interact with the product. The main chip used for this project is the ESP32 as it is able to support I2C which is needed for the project.

Figure 1: OLED schematic.

docs.title: Resouces
docs.text: The schematic as a PDF download is available here, and the Zip folder of the project here.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This page will go over the selection of the microcontroller used for the HMI module. It would focus on the ESP32 and how the configuration were made to ensure connectivity for the OLED and communication for the Team 307's Submersible Exploration Device.

docs.title: ESP32 Table of Contents
docs.text: ESP Info Answer ESP32-S3-WROOM-1-N8R8 Product Link Datasheet ESP32-S3-WROOM-1 Vendor Espressif Systems Unit cost $6.13/each Supply Voltage Range 3V ~ 3.6V Absolute Maximum current 500mA Maximum GPIO current  (per pin) 40mA Supports External Interrupts? Yes (All GPIOs) Programming Hardware Micropython

docs.title: Pin layout for ESP32
docs.text: Module # Available Needed Associated Pins UART 3 1 Rx - RXD0 and Tx - TXD0 I2C 38 2 IO15 and IO16 GPIO 38 8 IO6/IO7/IO11/IO12/IO13/IO14/IO46/IO23 LED 38 2 IO10 and IO5 USB Programmer 2 2 IO19 and IO20

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The point of this power budget is to estimate the power required to operate the HMI Module based off the components selected from the Component Selection page. Team 307 will be sharing a 12V of power which is displayed within the document while also showing which components would use the 3.3V and how much estimate current will be drawn. There is no battery used currently which is why that external power source 2 is left blank.

docs.title: Conclusions
docs.text: From the prepare Power Budget, the HMI module will be able to safely function as it meets the power requirements of each compinent. Based on the numbers, the 3.3V regulator can have a load between 537mA to 671mA (this is based off the 25% safety margin). This is proven to be operational based of the voltage regulator's datasheet. Thanks to that, all supporting components can safely operate.

docs.title: Resources
docs.text: The power budget as a PDF download is available here, and a Microsoft Excel Sheet here.
